category_title: Learners with ADD who overestimate their emotional competence and struggle with social-emotional processing
minimal_context: The student and tutor are reviewing a self-report measure of emotional intelligence alongside feedback from the student's teacher and mother during a semi-structured interview.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We are going to review your self-report measure and discuss the feedback provided by your teacher and your mother.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I already know I did well. I appraised myself as having higher emotional intelligence than my peers who did not have ADD.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at what your teacher wrote here about your interactions in class.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't agree with my teacher's perceptions of my emotional states.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Here is the feedback your mother provided about your behavior at home.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I do not show agreement with either my individual teachers or my individual mothers’ perceptions of my emotional states or my symptoms and behaviour! My emotional intelligence is at a higher level than that of my peer group.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We are going to review your self-report measure and discuss the feedback provided by your teacher and your mother.

STUDENT: I already know I did well. I appraised myself as having higher emotional intelligence than my peers who did not have ADD.

TUTOR: Let's look at what your teacher wrote here about your interactions in class.

STUDENT: I don't agree with my teacher's perceptions of my emotional states.

TUTOR: Here is the feedback your mother provided about your behavior at home.

STUDENT: I do not show agreement with either my individual teachers or my individual mothers’ perceptions of my emotional states or my symptoms and behaviour! My emotional intelligence is at a higher level than that of my peer group.

context.social_setting: individual teachers or their individual mothers
context.time_context: At the beginning of the study

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: evaluating themselves with regard to emotional intelligence
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: did not show agreement with either their individual teachers or their individual mothers’ perceptions of their emotional states or their symptoms and behaviour
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: displayed an unrealistically high perception of their emotional intelligence
related_quotes: did not show agreement with either their individual teachers or their individual mothers’ perceptions of their emotional states or their symptoms and behaviour

behavior_patterns.condition: evaluating themselves with regard to emotional intelligence
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: appraised themselves as having higher emotional intelligence than their peers who did not have ADD
behavior_patterns.internal_state: inaccurate appraisal of their emotional intelligence as being at a higher level than that of their peer group
